text: Thus, VATS has been proposed by some authors as an alternative to improve the complete tumor resections in this setting of patients.
After the complete removal of the diaphragmatic disease, a 3 to 5 cm hole is performed in the diaphragm if it was not opened during the peritoneal resection.
If no pleural diffusion is identified, a pleural cytology is performed with saline solution and sent for final pathology diagnosis.
If an implant is diagnosed, it can be resected at the same time if it is feasible.
The diaphragm is then closed by using a running suture while the air in the pleural space is aspirated and the anesthesiologist expands the lung.
The water test is finally done to confirm the appropriate closure of the diaphragm.
